pr.title: fix: social channel security & error handling (WeChat/Weibo verify)
pr.body: ## Summary Fixes 3 bugs in the WeChat/Weibo social channel verification code: 1. **Timing attack prevention** — `VerifyWeChatSignature` and `VerifyWeiboSignature` in `internal/domain/im/service_social.go` now use `crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare` instead of `==` to compare signatures, preventing timing side-channel attacks. 2. **WeChatVerify error handling** — The `WeChatVerify` handler in `internal/interfaces/http/handler/social_channel.go` now checks and handles the error returned by `MarkVerified`, returning a 500 if it fails. 3. **WeiboVerify validation** — The `WeiboVerify` handler was previously just echoing back `echostr` with no validation. It now: - Looks up the channel config - Verifies the request signature using `VerifyWeiboSignature` - Calls `MarkVerified` (with error handling) ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] Verify `VerifyWeChatSignature` and `VerifyWeiboSignature` correctly use `subtle.ConstantTimeCompare` — confirm existing unit tests still pass - [ ] Verify `WeChatVerify` handler returns 500 when `MarkVerified` fails (e.g., DB error) - [ ] Verify `WeiboVerify` handler rejects requests with missing/invalid channel config (404) or bad signature (403) - [ ] Test the full WeChat/Weibo webhook verification flow end-to-end in a staging environment ### Notes - All existing tests pass (`go test ./...` clean). - `go vet ./...` clean. - The `WeiboVerify` signature input uses `token + timestamp + nonce` concatenation, matching the Weibo verification protocol convention. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/ff3457a710a14521882dd7449645989b Requested by: @divord97 ---
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
